segments.text: Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven oxen and seven rams.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And Balak did as Balaam had spoken; and Balak and Balaam offered on every altar a bullock and a ram.
Stand by thy burnt offering, and I will go: peradventure the LORD will come to meet me: and whatsoever he sheweth me I will tell thee.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And he went to an high place.
And God met Balaam:
I have prepared seven altars, and I have offered upon every altar a bullock and a ram.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Return unto Balak, and thus thou shalt speak.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he returned unto him, and, lo, he stood by his burnt sacrifice, he, and all the princes of Moab.
Balak the king of Moab hath brought me from Aram, out of the mountains of the east, saying, Come, curse me Jacob, and come, defy Israel.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: How shall I curse, whom God hath not cursed? or how shall I defy, whom the LORD hath not defied?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: For from the top of the rocks I see him, and from the hills I behold him: lo, the people shall dwell alone, and shall not be reckoned among the nations.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Who can count the dust of Jacob, and the number of the fourth part of Israel? Let me die the death of the righteous, and let my last end be like his!
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: me: Heb. my soul, or, my life

segments.text: What hast thou done unto me? I took thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast blessed them altogether.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Must I not take heed to speak that which the LORD hath put in my mouth?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Come, I pray thee, with me unto another place, from whence thou mayest see them: thou shalt see but the utmost part of them, and shalt not see them all: and curse me them from thence.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: And he brought him into the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, and built seven altars, and offered a bullock and a ram on every altar.
Stand here by thy burnt offering, while I meet the Lord yonder.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Go again unto Balak, and say thus.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And when he came to him, behold, he stood by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him.
What hath the LORD spoken?
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Rise up, Balak, and hear; hearken unto me, thou son of Zippor:
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: God is not a man, that he should lie; neither the son of man, that he should repent: hath he said, and shall he not do it? or hath he spoken, and shall he not make it good?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Behold, I have received commandment to bless: and he hath blessed; and I cannot reverse it.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: He hath not beheld iniquity in Jacob, neither hath he seen perverseness in Israel: the LORD his God is with him, and the shout of a king is among them.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: God brought them out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Surely there is no enchantment against Jacob, neither is there any divination against Israel: according to this time it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel, What hath God wrought!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Behold, the people shall rise up as a great lion, and lift up himself as a young lion: he shall not lie down until he eat of the prey, and drink the blood of the slain.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Neither curse them at all, nor bless them at all.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Told not I thee, saying, All that the LORD speaketh, that I must do?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Come, I pray thee, I will bring thee unto another place; peradventure it will please God that thou mayest curse me them from thence.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: And Balak brought Balaam unto the top of Peor, that looketh toward Jeshimon.
Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven bullocks and seven rams.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bullock and a ram on every altar.
